questions.q: In which Indian state is the Bhojeshwar Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Bhojeshwar Temple stands at Bhojpur in the Raisen district of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: To which Hindu deity is the Bhojeshwar Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to Shiva and houses one of the largest stone lingams in India.

questions.q: The Bhojeshwar Temple is most famous for being in what state of completion?
questions.explanation: The temple was never completed, and it remains famous as a grand unfinished structure.

questions.q: After which king is the Bhojeshwar Temple named?
questions.explanation: The temple and the town of Bhojpur are named after the Paramara king Bhoja, who commissioned the temple.

questions.q: What is the single most notable object enshrined inside the Bhojeshwar Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple houses an enormous Shiva lingam carved from stone, one of the largest of its kind in India.

questions.q: Near which city is the Bhojeshwar Temple situated?
questions.explanation: Bhojpur, where the temple stands, lies a short distance southeast of Bhopal, the capital of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: What is the village or town where the Bhojeshwar Temple is located called?
questions.explanation: The temple is located in the town of Bhojpur, founded and named after King Bhoja.

questions.q: With which broad religion is the Bhojeshwar Temple associated?
questions.explanation: As a Shiva temple, Bhojeshwar is a Hindu place of worship.

questions.q: Because of its grand Shiva lingam and scale, the Bhojeshwar Temple has popularly been called the what?
questions.explanation: Because of its grand Shiva lingam and scale, the temple has been popularly called the 'Somnath of the East.'

questions.q: The main material used to build the Bhojeshwar Temple is which of the following?
questions.explanation: The temple was constructed from large blocks of sandstone quarried locally.

questions.q: To which dynasty did King Bhoja, the temple's patron, belong?
questions.explanation: Bhoja was the most celebrated ruler of the Paramara dynasty of Malwa.

questions.q: In which century is the Bhojeshwar Temple generally believed to have been built?
questions.explanation: The temple is dated to the 11th century CE, the period of King Bhoja's reign.

questions.q: From what is the great Shiva lingam of the Bhojeshwar Temple made?
questions.explanation: The colossal lingam is built from three superimposed (stacked) limestone blocks, and is one of the largest stone lingams in India.

questions.q: The Shiva lingam stands on a large platform known by which term?
questions.explanation: The colossal lingam rests on a massive square stone platform, the yoni-pitha, forming the base of the linga assembly.

questions.q: Which architectural style does the Bhojeshwar Temple represent?
questions.explanation: The temple is built in the Nagara style of North Indian temple architecture, with a planned curvilinear shikhara.

questions.q: A distinctive feature of the temple's roof is that it was planned to be capped by what, though it was never finished?
questions.explanation: The sanctum was intended to be crowned by a soaring Nagara-style shikhara that was never completed.

questions.q: What unusual feature near the temple offers rare evidence of medieval Indian construction methods?
questions.explanation: A large earthen ramp beside the temple, used to haul stones up during construction, still survives and reveals how medieval builders raised heavy blocks.

questions.q: Engraved architectural drawings found on the rocks around Bhojpur are significant because they show what?
options: Master plans and templates for the temple
questions.explanation: Architectural plans and templates incised into the surrounding rock surfaces provide rare insight into how the temple was designed and built.

questions.q: The temple stands on the bank of which river?
questions.explanation: The Bhojeshwar Temple overlooks the Betwa River at Bhojpur.

questions.q: Which heritage body is responsible for protecting and maintaining the Bhojeshwar Temple today?
options: Indian National Trust for Art and Cultural Heritage
questions.explanation: The temple is a protected monument under the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: The sanctum (garbhagriha) of the Bhojeshwar Temple is notable for being supported by how many great pillars?
questions.explanation: The sanctum is supported by four enormous pillars arranged to carry the planned roof and dome.

questions.q: King Bhoja, associated with this temple, is also famous in Indian tradition as a what?
options: Conqueror who never lost a battle
questions.explanation: Bhoja is remembered as a polymath scholar-king credited with works on many subjects and great patronage of arts and learning.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the great Shiva lingam at the Bhojeshwar Temple (the shaft above its platform)?
questions.explanation: The lingam shaft rises about 2.3 metres (7.5 feet) and is around 5.4 metres in circumference, resting on a large square platform; it is among the largest stone lingams in India.

questions.q: King Bhoja, who commissioned the temple, ruled over which historical region of central India?
questions.explanation: Bhoja was the Paramara ruler of Malwa, with his capital traditionally placed at Dhar.

questions.q: Besides the temple, King Bhoja is credited at Bhojpur with creating what major engineering work that was later destroyed?
questions.explanation: Bhoja is said to have built an enormous artificial lake near Bhojpur by damming the Betwa, later breached and drained in the medieval period.

questions.q: What is the principal scholarly explanation for why the Bhojeshwar Temple was left unfinished?
options: Construction halted, likely due to the decline of Bhoja's power and resources
A flood washed away the foundations
It was abandoned for being too small
questions.explanation: Work is believed to have stopped when the political fortunes and resources of Bhoja's Paramara kingdom declined, leaving the temple incomplete.

questions.q: The Bhojeshwar Temple's design is significant to historians because completed temples of the era rarely preserve what?
options: Evidence of the construction process itself
questions.explanation: Because it was abandoned mid-construction, the site uniquely preserves ramps, unfinished blocks, and engraved plans showing how a great medieval temple was actually built.

questions.q: What is the UNESCO World Heritage status of the Bhojeshwar Temple?
options: A standalone World Heritage Site since the 1980s
Not inscribed as a World Heritage Site (an ASI-protected monument)
Part of the Khajuraho group listing
questions.explanation: The Bhojeshwar Temple is a nationally protected ASI monument but is not inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List.

questions.q: The doorway and entrance of the sanctum at Bhojpur are notable for being unusually what for an Indian temple?
options: Very large and richly carved in scale
questions.explanation: The temple's sanctum doorway is exceptionally large and elaborately carved, in keeping with the monument's colossal intended scale.

questions.q: Carvings of which figures appear among the sculptural decoration of the Bhojeshwar Temple's sanctum?
questions.explanation: The sanctum's carved decoration includes Hindu divinities and amorous couple (mithuna) figures typical of Nagara temple ornamentation.
